chapter_title.en: Chapter 196 – Hastening of the Punishment for the sins

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/335)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/335)
text: إِذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِعَبْدٍ خَيْراً عَجَّلَ لَهُ عُقُوبَتَهُ فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ إِذَا أَرَادَ بِعَبْدٍ سُوءاً أَمْسَكَ عَلَيْهِ ذُنُوبَهُ حَتَّى يُوَافِيَ بِهَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Al Sary Bin Khalid,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Whenever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic Wants good with a servant, would Hasten his Punishment to him in the world; and whenever He<sup>azwj</sup> Wants Wrath upon a servant, would Withhold (Punishment) from him for his sins until He<sup>azwj</sup> gets these to be fulfilled with on the Day of Judgment’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from al-Sariy ibn Khalid from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “When Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, wants good for a servant He hastens his punishment in this world. When He wants evil for a servant He preserves his sins for him to find their consequences on the Day of Judgment.”
